api: Sage HR API v1.0 (https://developers.sage.hr/; OpenAPI spec mirrored at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/api-evangelist/sage-hr/main/openapi/sage-hr-openapi.yml, fetched 2026-07-03 since developers.sage.hr/apidoc.sage.hr itself return Cloudflare bot-challenge 403s to automated fetches)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the complete documented Sage HR v1.0 OpenAPI spec (47 paths). 14 read streams covering employees, teams, time off/leave management (requests, policies, KIT days, out-of-office, individual allowance reports), terminated employees, termination reasons, positions, recruitment positions and per-position applicants (fan_out), and onboarding/offboarding/document task categories. 10 write actions covering employee create/update/termination, employee custom-field update, time off request creation, KIT day creation and status transitions, leave policy KIT-day configuration, and onboarding/offboarding task creation. Excluded: multipart/file-upload endpoints (documents, recruitment applicant creation with resume attachment) the dialect cannot express (body_type has no multipart/binary support), the deeply-nested dynamic-date-keyed timesheets clock-in payload, the entire Vikarina payroll-bridge integration surface (10 endpoints; a separate third-party payroll product's data-transfer bridge, not a Sage HR data resource), single-object detail endpoints that duplicate an already-covered list stream's record shape, and read-only aggregate/reporting snapshots with no per-record identity (performance goal progress overviews).

excluded.reason: single-employee detail; identical record shape to the employees list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

per-employee compensation history sub-resource; a fan_out read over every employee id would require N employee-scoped requests per sync for a low-cardinality, rarely-changing field set — Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
per-employee custom-field value sub-resource; a fan_out read over every employee id would require N employee-scoped requests per sync — Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
per-employee leave-balance sub-resource; a fan_out read over every employee id would require N employee-scoped requests per sync — Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single-terminated-employee detail; identical record shape to the terminated_employees list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-policy detail; identical record shape to the leave_policies list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

requires BOTH a policy_id and an employee_id as required query filters with no listing/discovery endpoint for either dimension independent of the other; not a syncable top-level collection without externally-supplied ids in hand

single-recruitment-position detail; identical record shape to the recruitment_positions list stream's per-item object (plus an embedded applicants array already covered by the recruitment_applicants fan_out stream), already covered
multipart/form-data body carrying an optional resume/attachment file upload (applicant[attachment]); the dialect's body_type has no multipart/binary support

no top-level list/search endpoint exists to enumerate applicant ids independent of a specific recruitment position; applicants are already reachable via the recruitment_applicants fan_out stream, which is the syncable discovery path
per-applicant audit-log/timeline sub-resource; a second-level fan_out (over every applicant returned by the recruitment_applicants fan_out) is beyond this pass's breadth-vs-cost triage
multipart/form-data body carrying a required file upload (file); the dialect's body_type has no multipart/binary support
org-wide quarterly goal-progress aggregate snapshot with no per-record identity, not a syncable object stream
org-wide quarterly goal-progress aggregate snapshot with no per-record identity, not a syncable object stream
org-wide quarterly goal-progress aggregate snapshot with no per-record identity, not a syncable object stream
a single scalar-nested rollup object (company/individual/team adjusted-vs-actual counts), not a syncable object stream

request body is a dynamic date-string-keyed nested object (clocked_time: {"YYYY/MM/DD": {employee_id: [{clock_in, clock_out}, ...]}}); the dialect's body_type (json/form/none over a fixed field set) cannot express an arbitrary caller-supplied key structure
Vikarina payroll-bridge integration endpoint; transfers Sage HR data INTO a third-party payroll product, not a Sage HR data resource in its own right
